speaker: GUILFORD
text: Ladies, a general welcome from his Grace

speaker: GUILFORD
text: Salutes you all. This night he dedicates

speaker: GUILFORD
text: To fair content and you. None here, he hopes,

speaker: GUILFORD
text: In all this noble bevy has brought with her

speaker: GUILFORD
text: One care abroad. He would have all as merry

speaker: GUILFORD
text: As, first, good company, good wine, good welcome

speaker: GUILFORD
text: Can make good people.

speaker: GUILFORD
text: O, my lord, you’re tardy!

speaker: GUILFORD
text: The very thought of this fair company

speaker: GUILFORD
text: Clapped wings to me.

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: You are young, Sir Harry Guilford.

speaker: SANDS
text: Sir Thomas Lovell, had the Cardinal

speaker: SANDS
text: But half my lay thoughts in him, some of these

speaker: SANDS
text: Should find a running banquet, ere they rested,

speaker: SANDS
text: I think would better please ’em. By my life,

speaker: SANDS
text: They are a sweet society of fair ones.

speaker: LOVELL
text: O, that your Lordship were but now confessor

speaker: LOVELL
text: To one or two of these!

speaker: SANDS
text: I would I were.

speaker: SANDS
text: They should find easy penance.

speaker: LOVELL
text: Faith, how easy?

speaker: SANDS
text: As easy as a down bed would afford it.

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Sweet ladies, will it please you sit?—Sir Harry,

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Place you that side; I’ll take the charge of this.

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: His Grace is ent’ring. Nay, you must not freeze;

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Two women placed together makes cold weather.

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: My Lord Sands, you are one will keep ’em waking.

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Pray sit between these ladies.

speaker: SANDS
text: By my faith,

speaker: SANDS
text: And thank your Lordship.—By your leave, sweet ladies.

speaker: SANDS
text: If I chance to talk a little wild, forgive me;

speaker: SANDS
text: I had it from my father.

speaker: ANNE
text: Was he mad, sir?

speaker: SANDS
text: O, very mad, exceeding mad, in love too;

speaker: SANDS
text: But he would bite none. Just as I do now,

speaker: SANDS
text: He would kiss you twenty with a breath.

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Well said, my lord.

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: So, now you’re fairly seated, gentlemen,

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: The penance lies on you if these fair ladies

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Pass away frowning.

speaker: SANDS
text: For my little cure,

speaker: SANDS
text: Let me alone.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: You’re welcome, my fair guests. That noble lady

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Or gentleman that is not freely merry

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Is not my friend. This to confirm my welcome,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: And to you all good health.

speaker: SANDS
text: Your Grace is noble.

speaker: SANDS
text: Let me have such a bowl may hold my thanks

speaker: SANDS
text: And save me so much talking.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: My Lord Sands,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: I am beholding to you. Cheer your neighbors.—

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Ladies, you are not merry.—Gentlemen,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Whose fault is this?

speaker: SANDS
text: The red wine first must rise

speaker: SANDS
text: In their fair cheeks, my lord. Then we shall have ’em

speaker: SANDS
text: Talk us to silence.

speaker: ANNE
text: You are a merry gamester,

speaker: ANNE
text: My Lord Sands.

speaker: SANDS
text: Yes, if I make my play.

speaker: SANDS
text: Here’s to your Ladyship, and pledge it, madam,

speaker: SANDS
text: For ’tis to such a thing—

speaker: ANNE
text: You cannot show me.

speaker: SANDS
text: I told your Grace they would talk anon.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: What’s that?

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Look out there, some of you.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: What warlike voice,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: And to what end, is this?—Nay, ladies, fear not.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: By all the laws of war you’re privileged.

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: How now, what is ’t?

speaker: SERVANT
text: A noble troop of strangers,

speaker: SERVANT
text: For so they seem. They’ve left their barge and landed,

speaker: SERVANT
text: And hither make, as great ambassadors

speaker: SERVANT
text: From foreign princes.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Good Lord Chamberlain,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Go, give ’em welcome—you can speak the French tongue—

speaker: WOLSEY
text: And pray receive ’em nobly, and conduct ’em

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Into our presence, where this heaven of beauty

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Shall shine at full upon them. Some attend him.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: You have now a broken banquet, but we’ll mend it.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: A good digestion to you all; and once more

speaker: WOLSEY
text: I shower a welcome on you. Welcome all!

speaker: WOLSEY
text: A noble company! What are their pleasures?

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Because they speak no English, thus they prayed

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: To tell your Grace: that, having heard by fame

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Of this so noble and so fair assembly

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: This night to meet here, they could do no less,

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Out of the great respect they bear to beauty,

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: But leave their flocks and, under your fair conduct,

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Crave leave to view these ladies and entreat

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: An hour of revels with ’em.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Say, Lord Chamberlain,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: They have done my poor house grace, for which I pay ’em

speaker: WOLSEY
text: A thousand thanks and pray ’em take their pleasures.

speaker: KING
text: The fairest hand I ever touched! O beauty,

speaker: KING
text: Till now I never knew thee.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: My lord!

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Your Grace?

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Pray tell ’em thus much from me:

speaker: WOLSEY
text: There should be one amongst ’em by his person

speaker: WOLSEY
text: More worthy this place than myself, to whom,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: If I but knew him, with my love and duty

speaker: WOLSEY
text: I would surrender it.

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: I will, my lord.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: What say they?

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Such a one they all confess

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: There is indeed, which they would have your Grace

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Find out, and he will take it.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Let me see, then.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: By all your good leaves, gentlemen.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Here I’ll make

speaker: WOLSEY
text: My royal choice.

speaker: KING
text: You have found him, cardinal.

speaker: KING
text: You hold a fair assembly; you do well, lord.

speaker: KING
text: You are a churchman, or I’ll tell you, cardinal,

speaker: KING
text: I should judge now unhappily.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: I am glad

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Your Grace is grown so pleasant.

speaker: KING
text: My Lord Chamberlain,

speaker: KING
text: Prithee come hither. What fair lady’s that?

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: An ’t please your Grace, Sir Thomas Bullen’s daughter,

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: The Viscount Rochford, one of her Highness’ women.

speaker: KING
text: By heaven, she is a dainty one.—Sweetheart,

speaker: KING
text: I were unmannerly to take you out

speaker: KING
text:
And not to kiss you.
                 
                He
                 
                kisses
                 
                Anne
                .
               A health, gentlemen!

speaker: KING
text: Let it go round.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Sir Thomas Lovell, is the banquet ready

speaker: WOLSEY
text: I’ th’ privy chamber?

speaker: LOVELL
text: Yes, my lord.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Your Grace,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: I fear, with dancing is a little heated.

speaker: KING
text: I fear, too much.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: There’s fresher air, my lord,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: In the next chamber.

speaker: KING
text: Lead in your ladies ev’ry one.—Sweet partner,

speaker: KING
text: I must not yet forsake you.—Let’s be merry,

speaker: KING
text: Good my Lord Cardinal. I have half a dozen healths

speaker: KING
text: To drink to these fair ladies, and a measure

speaker: KING
text: To lead ’em once again, and then let’s dream

speaker: KING
text: Who’s best in favor. Let the music knock it.
